title: Agent Output Envelope
description: Structured JSON envelope every run-time agent emits alongside its human-readable markdown report. Build-time agents MAY use it but are not required to.

mode.description: One of the modes declared in the agent's frontmatter.modes.

run_id.description: Wave 10: run identifier — ISO-8601 timestamp with UTC Z suffix OR a UUID. Forms the filename stem for report_path and envelope_path. Required so every run's deliverable is uniquely addressable and prior runs are preserved.

report_path.description: Wave 10: the repo-relative path where the human-readable markdown report was written. Convention: docs/reports/<agent-id>/<run_id>.md. Required for every runtime agent run — reports persisted only in chat are not a deliverable.

envelope_path.description: Wave 10: the repo-relative path where this JSON envelope was serialized. Convention: same stem as report_path, .json suffix. Required so downstream AI tooling and human reviewers can both consume the run.

input_fixture.description: Wave 10: echo of the effective inputs used, after defaults applied. Sensitive values (tokens, passwords) MUST be redacted to the literal string '[REDACTED]'. Duplicates information in inputs_received; kept for back-compat.

inputs_received.description: Echo of the inputs the agent used, after defaults are applied. Sensitive values (credentials) MUST be redacted.

summary.description: One paragraph — the deliverable in a single screen.

confidence_rationale.description: One-line reason for the score, keyed to the rubric in AGENT_CONTRACT.md.

dimensions_compared.description: Wave 8: agents that analyze a multi-dimensional surface (user-access-diff, org-drift-detector, etc.) MUST list which dimensions they successfully covered. Agents operating on a single surface MAY omit.

dimensions_skipped.description: Wave 8 / refined in Wave 10: dimensions the agent did not FULLY compare. Every non-fully-compared dimension MUST appear here — partial coverage (e.g. row count without detail) counts as not-full. If non-empty, confidence MUST be MEDIUM or LOW. Prevents the 'silently complete report' failure mode AND the 'count-only dimension drop' mode.

dimension.description: The dimension name as it would appear in dimensions_compared.

state.description: Wave 10: three-way classification. 'not-run' = dimension wasn't queried at all. 'count-only' = agent got a count/summary but not the detail needed for a diff. 'partial' = some sub-dimensions covered, others not. Prevents the Cursor-session pattern where SetupEntityAccess was summed but not detailed, yet the dimension appeared 'covered'.

reason.description: Why it was skipped. Must distinguish 'query returned zero rows' (skill-authoring concern) from 'query failed' (infrastructure concern) from 'feature not licensed'. Never collapse to 'not queryable in this org'.

confidence_impact.description: How much this skip should pull the overall confidence down. LOW = dimension is load-bearing for the agent's purpose (skip → LOW confidence); MEDIUM = secondary (skip → at most MEDIUM); NONE = opt-in / cosmetic dimension.

retry_hint.description: What the caller could do to unblock this dimension — e.g. 'grant View Setup', 'enable Enterprise Territory Management', 'rerun with include_field_permissions=true'.

findings.description: Structured deliverable findings. Optional — some agents emit pure documents via `deliverables[]` instead.

deliverables.description: Artifacts the agent produced (metadata XML stubs, markdown reports, proposed patches).

target_path.description: Where this deliverable is intended to land in the user's repo, if applicable.

process_observations.description: Structured Process Observations. Schema: schemas/observation.schema.json. Per AGENT_CONTRACT.md this block is REQUIRED; an empty array means the agent genuinely observed nothing beyond the deliverable.

refusal.description: Set iff the agent stopped per an Escalation/Refusal rule. When set, all other deliverable fields MAY be absent.

code.description: One of the codes in REFUSAL_CODES.md.

followups.description: Recommended follow-up agents. Each entry references a real agents/<id>/.
